tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, whether the subject had developed cancer, age when blood sample was collected, mutated gene, and gene expression identifier.

variables.name: age
variables.description: Age when blood sample was collected

variables.name: developed_cancer
variables.description: Whether the subject had developed cancer

variables.name: gene_expression_id
variables.description: Identifier that indicates sample ID for gene-expression data in GEO

variables.name: mutated_gene
variables.description: Gene in which a known pathogenic variant had been identified

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample ID, body site where sample was collected, analyte type, tumor status, and histological type.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject ID, consent information, and case control status for breast cancer.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status for breast cancer

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample use.

variables.name: SAMPLE_USE
variables.description: Sample use. Seq_DNA_SNP_CNV: SNP and CNV genotypes derived from sequence data; Seq_DNA_WholeExome: Whole exome sequencing
